title: ruta chalepensis var. bracteosa
intro: Ruta chalepensis var. bracteosa, also known as common rue, is a perennial plant in the rutaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ruta chalepensis var. bracteosa include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
